concept: Would you craft an adventure game where the player character can detach and reattach their own limbs, using them as tools to solve puzzles throughout the world? Each limb has unique abilities when detached - arms can crawl into small spaces, legs can be stacked to reach high places, and the head can roll through narrow passages while still allowing the player to see. The environment contains various machines and mechanisms specifically designed to be operated by detached body parts placed in particular configurations. Some areas can only be navigated while missing certain limbs, forcing players to adapt their movement techniques accordingly. The protagonist is searching for the mythical complete body that will finally make them whole after existing as a patchwork creation of mismatched parts.
